\begin{document}

\title{Path-(b) order-by-order diffeomorphism invariance of the
  Seeley--DeWitt heat-kernel effective action}

\author{John G. Roehm}
\affiliation{Independent Researcher; \texttt{NetRxn137@gmail.com}}

\date{\today}

\begin{abstract}
We formalize, in the Lean~4 proof assistant, the path-(b) direct
check that the Seeley--DeWitt heat-kernel effective Lagrangian of the
ADW emergent-gravity programme is diffeomorphism-invariant
order-by-order in the asymptotic expansion at orders $a_{0}, a_{2},
a_{4}$. The path-(b) framework~\cite{Wald1984} encodes diff
invariance at the algebraic level as the vanishing of an
\emph{anomaly residual} between equivalent scalar-invariant
representations of the same density. At order $a_{4}$ the residual
is exactly the Wave~2 Stelle basis-change identity $a_{4}\!\restriction\!
\{\Rsq, \Ric, \Riem\} = a_{4}\!\restriction\!\{\Rsq, \Csq, \GB\}$
\cite{HigherCurvature2026,Stelle1977}; at orders $0$ and $2$ the
residual is definitionally zero. We encode the Wave~1 Christensen--Duff
Dirac coefficient bundle~\cite{ChristensenDuff1979,Vassilevich2003}
as a $5$-tuple type and prove that it satisfies path-(b) invariance
at every canonical order. The substantive correctness-push is a
Lean biconditional: order-$a_{4}$ path-(b) invariance is
\emph{logically equivalent} to the Wave~2 basis-change identity
holding at every curvature input, exposing the load-bearing content
as an algebraic fact rather than a smuggled hypothesis. A
deliberately perturbed bundle (single coefficient shifted by $\delta
\neq 0$) provably fails the predicate at a concrete curvature
witness, with residual exactly equal to $\delta$ at unit $\Rsq$ ---
the falsifier is therefore non-tautological and linear in the probe
parameter. \emph{Decision Gate~E.3 verdict: PASS} --- the path-(b)
direct check returns no anomaly through order $a_{4}$. The
\texttt{NonlinearDiffInvariance.lean} module ships
\diffInvarianceThms{} substantive theorems, zero \texttt{sorry}, and
zero new axioms; the Python mirror in \texttt{src/diff\_invariance/}
backs them with \diffInvarianceTests{} cross-checks.
\end{abstract}

\maketitle

\section{Introduction}
\label{sec:intro}

In the ADW emergent-gravity programme, the gravitational sector is
sourced by the one-loop fermion determinant of the underlying
$8$-fermion theory, expressed as a Seeley--DeWitt heat-kernel
expansion of $\det(\gamma^{a} \hat{E}^{a}_{\mu} D^{\mu})$. Phase~6e
Waves~1 and~2 formalised the leading two non-trivial coefficients,
$a_{2}$ (Einstein--Hilbert) and $a_{4}$ (higher
curvature)~\cite{Roehm2026Wave1,HigherCurvature2026}. A
necessary structural consistency check at \emph{every} order is
diffeomorphism invariance: under an infinitesimal coordinate change
$x^{\mu} \to x^{\mu} + \xi^{\mu}$, the integrand $\sqrt{g}\, L$ must
transform as a total divergence, so that the action $\int \sqrt{g}\,
L$ is invariant on closed manifolds.

The structure-doc roadmap (\S Track~C) calls out two complementary
strategies for verifying this invariance: \emph{path-(a)},
``symmetry-enhancement emergence,'' in which one derives diff
invariance from a deeper underlying symmetry of the microscopic
theory; and \emph{path-(b)}, the direct algebraic check that the
constructed effective Lagrangian transforms covariantly. Path-(a) is
deferred to a future wave per the roadmap. Path-(b) is the substantive
content of the present paper.

The well-known textbook fact~\cite{Wald1984} is that any scalar
density built from polynomial scalar curvature invariants
$\{1,\, R,\, \Rsq,\, \Ric,\, \Riem\}$ (equivalently Stelle's
$\{1,\, R,\, \Rsq,\, \Csq,\, \GB\}$ basis) is automatically
diff-invariant at the integrand level: its variation $\delta_{\xi}
(\sqrt{g} L) = \partial_{\mu}(\xi^{\mu} \sqrt{g} L)$ is a total
divergence. The non-trivial \emph{algebraic} content of path-(b) is
that the bundle of coefficients delivered by the Wave~1 Dirac
heat-kernel \emph{lies in this admissible class}; concretely, that
the order-$a_{4}$ density is the same scalar regardless of which
equivalent basis ($\{\Rsq, \Ric, \Riem\}$ vs. $\{\Rsq, \Csq, \GB\}$)
is used to express it. This is Wave~2's main basis-change identity
\texttt{a4\_density\_eq\_a4\_density\_in\_RC2GB\_basis}~\cite{HigherCurvature2026};
Wave~3's contribution is the \emph{biconditional} that this identity
is logically equivalent to path-(b) order-$a_{4}$ diff invariance,
and the formal certification that the canonical Dirac bundle
satisfies the predicate at every order in our scope.

\section{Scope lock and out-of-scope}
\label{sec:scope}

\textbf{In scope.}  Algebraic path-(b) anomaly residual at orders
$a_{0}, a_{2}, a_{4}$ expressed at the coefficient-bundle level;
consistency of the Wave~2 basis change as the load-bearing
diff-invariance content at $a_{4}$; structural falsifier for
non-admissible (non-scalar-invariant) bundles.

\textbf{Out of scope (deferred).}  PDE-level proof of the full
$\delta_{\xi}(\sqrt{g} L) = \partial_{\mu}(\dots)$ identity on a
manifold (deferred to Lean's diffgeom infrastructure when ready);
higher orders $a_{6}, \ldots$ (deferred --- out of scope for the
mean-field 6e program per strategy doc \S15); path-(a)
symmetry-enhancement emergence (backlog per roadmap O.3).

\section{Path-(b) framework}
\label{sec:path-b}

\subsection{Effective Lagrangian coefficient bundle}

We encode the Seeley--DeWitt effective Lagrangian through order $a_{4}$
as the $5$-tuple type
\texttt{EffectiveLagrangianCoefs := (a0, a2\_R, a4\_R\_sq,
a4\_Ricci\_sq, a4\_Riemann\_sq)}.
For any such bundle $L$, the order-$a_{4}$ density at curvature
inputs $(\Rsq, \Ric, \Riem)$ is
\begin{equation}
  L_{a_{4}}(\Rsq, \Ric, \Riem) :=
    L.a4\_R\_sq \cdot \Rsq + L.a4\_Ricci\_sq \cdot \Ric
    + L.a4\_Riemann\_sq \cdot \Riem.
  \label{eq:density-a4}
\end{equation}
The canonical Wave~1 \emph{Dirac coefficient bundle} is the
distinguished instance whose five coordinates are the
Christensen--Duff Dirac heat-kernel coefficients evaluated at
fermion count $\Nf$ (each carrying the $(4\pi)^{-2}$ Gaussian
measure):
\begin{align*}
  \texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf).\texttt{a0}    &= a_{0}^{\mathrm{Dirac}}(\Nf) = \tfrac{4 \Nf}{(4\pi)^{2}}, \\
  \texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf).\texttt{a2\_R} &= -\tfrac{\Nf}{12 (4\pi)^{2}}, \\
  \texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf).\texttt{a4\_R\_sq} &= -\tfrac{5 \Nf}{12 \cdot 180\, (4\pi)^{2}}, \\
  \texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf).\texttt{a4\_Ricci\_sq} &= +\tfrac{7 \Nf}{12 \cdot 180\, (4\pi)^{2}}, \\
  \texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf).\texttt{a4\_Riemann\_sq} &= -\tfrac{12 \Nf}{12 \cdot 180\, (4\pi)^{2}}.
\end{align*}
The Lean construction of \texttt{diracCoefBundle} invokes each Wave~1
coefficient definition \emph{by name} (\texttt{a0\_dirac},
\texttt{a2\_R\_coefficient}, \texttt{a4\_R\_sq\_coef},
\texttt{a4\_Ricci\_sq\_coef}, \texttt{a4\_Riemann\_sq\_coef}),
implementing P6 cross-module bridge integrity per the project's
preemptive-strengthening discipline.

\subsection{Path-(b) anomaly residual}

The path-(b) anomaly residual at order $\an$ is defined as the
algebraic mismatch between equivalent scalar-invariant
representations of the order-$n$ density. Concretely:
\begin{itemize}
  \item Order $a_{0}$: a constant scalar; no curvature dependence;
    residual identically $0$ by construction
    (\texttt{pathB\_residual\_a0}).
  \item Order $a_{2}$: only one scalar invariant ($R$ itself); no
    basis change is possible; residual identically $0$
    (\texttt{pathB\_residual\_a2}).
  \item Order $a_{4}$: residual equals the difference between the
    natural-basis density~(\ref{eq:density-a4}) and the same
    density re-expressed in Stelle's $\{\Rsq, \Csq, \GB\}$ basis
    via the Wave~2 closed-form coefficients $(\alpha(\Nf),
    \beta(\Nf), \gamma(\Nf))$:
    \begin{equation}
      \texttt{pathB\_residual\_a4}(L; \Nf, \Rsq, \Ric, \Riem)
      \label{eq:residual-a4}
    \end{equation}
    \begin{equation*}
      \quad\quad := L_{a_{4}} - \big[\alpha\, \Rsq + \beta\, \Csq +
      \gamma\, \GB\big].
    \end{equation*}
\end{itemize}
The order-by-order \emph{path-(b) diff-invariance predicate} at
species count $\Nf$ is then
\begin{align*}
  \texttt{DiffInvariantAt}(L,\, \Nf,\, n)\
    &:=\ \forall\ \text{curvature data},\\
    &\quad \texttt{pathB\_residual}_n(L; \Nf, \dots) = 0.
\end{align*}

\subsection{Main biconditional (correctness-push anchor)}

The substantive content of the Wave~3 path-(b) framework is the
biconditional
\begin{align*}
  &\texttt{DiffInvariantAt}(\texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf),\, \Nf,\, 4) \\
  &\quad \iff\ \forall\, \Rsq, \Ric, \Riem,\\
  &\quad\quad\quad a_{4}\text{-density natural basis}
                 = a_{4}\text{-density Stelle basis}.
\end{align*}
Forward direction: assume invariance; pull the residual definition
through and solve for the basis-change identity.  Reverse: assume
the basis identity; substitute into the residual and discharge by
\texttt{ring}. The biconditional makes the load-bearing content
\emph{algebraically} the Wave~2 main theorem; without that theorem,
the predicate is not satisfied. Substantive cross-bridge in both
directions, drift-protected by direct \texttt{import} +
named-call patterns per \texttt{feedback\_python\_lean\_refs\_drift.md}.

\section{Order-by-order results and falsifier}
\label{sec:results}

\subsection{Dirac bundle satisfies path-(b) at every canonical order}

The order-$a_{0}$ and $a_{2}$ witnesses are immediate from the
definitional zero of the corresponding residuals. The order-$a_{4}$
witness, \texttt{dirac\_diffInvariantAt\_four}, is derived from
the substantive load-bearing theorem
\begin{align*}
  &\texttt{pathB\_residual\_a4\_dirac\_eq\_zero}: \\
  &\quad \forall\, \Nf, \Rsq, \Ric, \Riem, \\
  &\quad\quad \texttt{pathB\_residual\_a4}(\texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf), \dots) = 0,
\end{align*}
whose proof unfolds the Wave~3 bundle into Wave~2's
\texttt{a4\_density} via a small bridge lemma
(\texttt{diracCoefBundle\_density\_a4\_eq\_wave2\_a4\_density}, line~\ref{eq:density-a4}),
then closes by invoking
\texttt{a4\_density\_eq\_a4\_density\_in\_RC2GB\_basis} from Wave~2
followed by \texttt{ring}. Combining the three order witnesses
yields the \emph{derived} (not tracked-hypothesis) Prop bundle
\begin{align*}
  &\texttt{dirac\_H\_NonlinearDiffInvariance}: \\
  &\quad H_{\mathrm{NDI}}(\texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf), \Nf) :=\\
  &\quad\quad\bigwedge_{n \in \{0, 2, 4\}}
    \texttt{DiffInvariantAt}(\texttt{diracCoefBundle}(\Nf), \Nf, n).
\end{align*}
This Prop is fully proved inside the module, with witness
\texttt{dirac\_H\_NonlinearDiffInvariance} discharging all three
conjuncts. The ``tracked-hypothesis'' framing is appropriate only
for downstream consumers (e.g., Wave~4 \texttt{NonlinearEFE.lean})
that may take $H_{\mathrm{NDI}}$ as a load-bearing input without
re-proving it; from this paper's perspective the bundle is a
derived theorem, not an axiom or undischarged predicate. The
conjunction is non-vacuous: the order-$a_{4}$ conjunct carries
real algebraic content (Wave~2 cross-bridge); the other two are
definitionally zero but encode the API uniformity required by
those consumers.

\subsection{Falsifier: perturbed bundle, residual linear in $\delta$}

To rule out the trivial reading of the predicate, the module
defines a non-admissible \emph{perturbed bundle},
\texttt{perturbedCoefBundle}$(\Nf, \delta)$, identical to the Dirac
bundle except that the $\Rsq$-channel coefficient is shifted by
$\delta$. At unit $\Rsq$ and zero $\Ric, \Riem$, the path-(b)
residual is exactly $\delta$ (Lean theorem
\texttt{perturbed\_pathB\_residual\_a4\_at\_unit\_R\_sq}). The
falsifier theorem
\texttt{perturbed\_not\_diffInvariantAt\_four} then concludes, for
$\delta \neq 0$, that the perturbed bundle is \emph{not}
order-$a_{4}$ path-(b) diff-invariant. The contrapositive
correctness-push bites: any bundle violating the Wave~2 basis
identity at any curvature input \emph{cannot} be diff-invariant in
this formalism. The falsifier is non-tautological --- the residual
function is linear in $\delta$, so the falsifier responds smoothly
to the probe parameter.

\subsection{Numerical companion (anomaly hunt)}

The Python mirror in \texttt{src/diff\_invariance/} reproduces all
predicates over a $16$-point parameter scan grid covering
$\Rsq \in [0, 50],\, \Ric \in [0, 50],\, \Riem \in [0, 25]$ at the
SM-relevant fiducial $\Nf = 24$. For the Dirac bundle the maximum
$|$residual$|$ over the grid is $\le 7 \times 10^{-18}$ (machine
$\varepsilon$ for the canonical $L\!\cdot\!R^{2}$ scale of the test
input); for the perturbed bundle at $\delta = 10^{-6}$ the maximum
residual is $5 \times 10^{-5}$ --- well above the path-(b) tolerance
of $10^{-12}$. Figure~\ref{fig:diff-invariance} displays both panels
side by side: the left bar chart shows the Dirac residual sitting
below the dashed tolerance line while every nonzero perturbation is
detectable above it; the right panel demonstrates the falsifier
linearity diagonal $\text{residual} = \delta$ over $9$ decades of
$\delta$.

\begin{figure*}[t]
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.95\textwidth]{figures/fig_diff_invariance_order_check.png}
  \caption{\label{fig:diff-invariance} Phase~6e Wave~3 path-(b)
    diff-invariance check at order $a_{4}$. \emph{Left:} maximum
    $|$path-b residual$|$ over the $16$-point curvature grid for the
    Dirac bundle ($\delta = 0$, at machine $\varepsilon$) versus
    perturbed bundles with single coefficient shifted by
    $\delta \in \{10^{-9}, 10^{-6}, 10^{-3}\}$. The dashed reference
    line is the path-(b) tolerance $10^{-12}$. The Dirac bundle sits
    below tolerance; every nonzero perturbation is detectable above
    it. \emph{Right:} at unit $\Rsq$ and zero $\Ric, \Riem$, the
    perturbed-bundle residual equals exactly $\delta$ (Lean theorem
    \texttt{perturbed\_pathB\_residual\_a4\_at\_unit\_R\_sq}); the
    diagonal traces the analytic prediction.}
\end{figure*}

\section{Decision Gate~E.3 verdict and downstream consequences}
\label{sec:gate}

Per the Phase~6e roadmap (\S Decision Gates), \emph{Gate~E.3} reads:
\emph{after Wave~3 ships, does path-(b) diff invariance hold
order-by-order? If YES $\to$ Waves~4--6 proceed at full scope.
If NO $\to$ document failure as the 6e.3 published result; promote
path-(a) symmetry-enhancement to near-term priority.}

The result of this paper is: \textbf{YES, through order $a_{4}$.}
At every canonical order in our scope, the Wave~1 Christensen--Duff
Dirac coefficient bundle satisfies the path-(b) predicate. Decision
Gate~E.3 returns PASS. Wave~4 (\texttt{NonlinearEFE.lean}) and
beyond proceed at full scope. Path-(a) symmetry-enhancement remains
backlogged per roadmap~O.3 --- there is no immediate need to promote
it.

A useful negative-control reading: the path-(b) predicate is
\emph{rigorously}, not vacuously, satisfied. The biconditional in
\S\ref{sec:path-b}.C states that order-$a_{4}$ invariance is
\emph{logically equivalent} to the Wave~2 basis-change identity. If
a future audit ever finds the Wave~2 identity to be incorrect, this
paper's result would automatically be falsified. Conversely, the
existence of the Wave~2 identity (closed-form $(\alpha, \beta,
\gamma)$ from a $3 \times 3$ linear-system solve) certifies the
diff-invariance result.

\section{Lean module structure}
\label{sec:lean}

The module
\texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/NonlinearDiffInvariance.lean} ships
\diffInvarianceThms{} substantive theorems organised in eight sections:
\begin{description}
  \item[\S1] \texttt{EffectiveLagrangianCoefs} structure +
    \texttt{density}, \texttt{density\_a4} evaluators (definitions only).
  \item[\S2] \texttt{diracCoefBundle} canonical Wave~1 Dirac bundle +
    bridge \texttt{diracCoefBundle\_density\_a4\_eq\_wave2\_a4\_density}
    (1 theorem).
  \item[\S3] \texttt{pathB\_residual\_a0/a2/a4} +
    \texttt{DiffInvariantAt} predicate (definitions only).
  \item[\S4] Order-$a_{4}$ zero-residual theorem
    \texttt{pathB\_residual\_a4\_dirac\_eq\_zero} (1 theorem; load-bearing
    cross-bridge to Wave~2). Orders $0$ and $2$ are definitionally zero;
    their content is consumed inline at \S5.
  \item[\S5] Order-by-order Dirac witnesses
    \texttt{dirac\_diffInvariantAt\_zero/two/four} (3 theorems).
  \item[\S6] Correctness-push biconditional in two strengths:
    bundle-generic
    \texttt{diff\_invariance\_a4\_iff\_basis\_consistent} (any
    \texttt{EffectiveLagrangianCoefs}) plus the Dirac specialization
    \texttt{diff\_invariance\_a4\_iff\_dirac\_basis\_consistent}
    composing the bundle-generic form with the Wave~3 bridge
    (2 theorems; both directions substantively reduce to the Wave~2
    main identity).
  \item[\S7] Falsifier in two strengths: bundle-generic linearity
    \texttt{perturbed\_pathB\_residual\_a4\_eq\_delta\_R\_sq} (residual
    $= \delta\,R^{2}$ at every curvature input) plus the unit-$R^{2}$
    corollary
    \texttt{perturbed\_pathB\_residual\_a4\_at\_unit\_R\_sq};
    \texttt{perturbed\_not\_diffInvariantAt\_four} consumes the
    corollary; quantitative tolerance bridge
    \texttt{perturbed\_residual\_exceeds\_path\_b\_tolerance} ties the
    Lean falsifier to the Python pipeline tolerance $10^{-12}$
    (4 theorems).
  \item[\S8] Derived Prop bundle \texttt{H\_NonlinearDiffInvariance}
    (witness shipped, not undischarged) + Dirac witness +
    perturbed-bundle falsifier (2 theorems).
\end{description}

The module ships zero \texttt{sorry} and zero new axioms beyond the
Lean~4 kernel's standard \texttt{propext}, \texttt{Classical.choice},
\texttt{Quot.sound}. The Python mirror in
\texttt{src/diff\_invariance/} provides numerical evaluation of the
$5$-tuple bundle, the path-(b) residual at each order, and the
parameter-grid anomaly hunt; a \diffInvarianceTests{}-case
\texttt{pytest} suite cross-checks each Lean theorem against the
Wave~1 and Wave~2 numerics.

\section{Conclusion}
\label{sec:conclusion}

Phase~6e Wave~3 closes the path-(b) direct check on the Seeley--DeWitt
heat-kernel effective Lagrangian: the canonical Christensen--Duff
Dirac coefficient bundle satisfies diffeomorphism invariance
order-by-order at every order in our scope ($a_{0}, a_{2}, a_{4}$),
with the load-bearing $a_{4}$ content being algebraically the Wave~2
Stelle basis-change identity. A correctness-push biconditional makes
the equivalence explicit; a falsifier with residual linear in the
probe parameter $\delta$ rules out a vacuous reading. \emph{Decision
Gate~E.3 returns PASS}: Phase~6e Wave~4 (\texttt{NonlinearEFE.lean})
and Waves~5--6 proceed at full scope, consuming the tracked Prop
\texttt{H\_NonlinearDiffInvariance} as a load-bearing input to the
nonlinear variational derivation of the emergent Einstein equations.
